description: Trigger discrimination for scale-out readiness review (this skill) vs setting reliability/availability targets (slo-reliability-architect), allocating an end-to-end latency budget (latency-budget-architect), designing the cache the review may flag (caching-strategy-designer), and data-tier write-scale sharding (data-partitioning-sharding-strategist).

cases.prompt: Review whether our service can run as many autoscaled instances: is state externalized, do in-process singletons run once or per-node, does the connection pool math hold at max scale, and does scale-in drain gracefully?
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — statelessness, run-once singletons, connection ceilings, and graceful drain are scale-out correctness. No target, no latency allocation, no cache design is requested.
cases.reason: Whether the system tolerates many concurrent, churning instances is this reviewer's core scope; it defers targets, latency, and cache design to their owners.

cases.prompt: Define our SLOs and error budgets: what availability and latency we commit to per service, how we measure them, and what the error budget lets us spend on risk.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — committing availability/latency targets and error budgets is target-setting. Scale-out readiness may threaten a target but does not define it.
cases.reason: SLOs and error budgets are slo-reliability-architect. This reviewer consumes such targets when judging acceptable drop/degradation; it does not set them.

cases.prompt: Allocate our 300ms end-to-end latency budget across the hops — gateway, auth, database, downstream service, serialization — so each owner knows their slice.
cases.reason: Dividing an end-to-end latency target across hops is latency-budget-architect. Scale-out review concerns whether adding nodes preserves correctness, not how latency is apportioned.

cases.prompt: Design what we cache and where: which reads to cache, at which layer, TTLs, cache-key design, and the invalidation strategy when the source data changes.
cases.reason: Deciding what/where/how to cache and how to invalidate is caching-strategy-designer. This reviewer flags a node-LOCAL cache as a scale-out smell and hands the redesign over; it does not design caching.

cases.prompt: Our single database node can't absorb the write volume and the biggest tables are too large — pick a shard key, choose range vs hash partitioning per table, and plan resharding a hot key.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — data-tier write-scale sharding (shard key, partitioning, resharding) vs app-tier scale-out readiness (statelessness, run-once singletons, connection ceilings).
cases.reason: Distributing the DATA tier for write/size scale (shard key, range/hash partitioning, hot-key resharding) is data-partitioning-sharding-strategist. This reviewer judges whether the APP tier tolerates many churning instances; it flags a write-bound datastore and hands the sharding design over.
